title: SQLite backup documented as operator responsibility (Litestream)
description: Document that database backup/durability is the operating team's responsibility (not built into SystemBook itself), with a concrete recommended setup using Litestream for continuous SQLite replication.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/backup.md` (or a section in TASK-60's setup docs) explicitly states SystemBook does not include built-in backup automation, and this is an intentional MVP scope boundary
Doc explains the risk in plain terms: data loss on volume loss without a backup strategy in place

Doc provides a concrete Litestream configuration example replicating `DATABASE_PATH` to an S3-compatible bucket (or local secondary path as a minimal alternative), including how to add it as a sidecar in the production compose file (TASK-59)
Doc includes a basic restore procedure (how to recover a Litestream-replicated database into a fresh instance)
Cross-linked from TASK-60's setup docs and TASK-62's README

steps.details: Add an explicit statement at the top of `docs/backup.md`: SystemBook stores all state in a single SQLite file; backup and disaster recovery are the operator's responsibility, not automated by the platform.

Document adding a `litestream` sidecar container to `docker-compose.production.yml` (TASK-59), configured with a `litestream.yml` replicating `DATABASE_PATH` to an example S3-compatible destination, with placeholder credentials clearly marked as such.
Document the `litestream restore` command sequence to recover a database file from the replicated destination into a fresh volume before starting the SystemBook container.

steps.description: Cross-link from setup docs and README
steps.details: Add links to `docs/backup.md` from TASK-60's setup guide (as a 'before going to production' step) and from TASK-62's README.

technicalNotes: Litestream is a documentation-only recommendation in the MVP — no code integration is required from SystemBook itself; it works purely by watching the SQLite file on disk.
